tweets.text: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps is close to joining London City Lionesses ✍️
England land in Miami ahead of the World Cup 🏆
BREAKING: Chelsea announce the signing of Ireland international Katie McCabe, with the talented left-back to join from 1 July 2026 upon the expiration of her current deal with Arsenal. 🚨

"I feel it's the right time"

Anthony Joshua gives an exclusive interview with Sky Sports about the potential fight with Tyson Fury 🥊
BREAKING: Celtic principal shareholder Dermot Desmond is due to meet with Martin O’Neill and Robbie Keane this week over the managerial vacancy. 🚨

"A brotherhood in the squad"

Rob Dorsett on the England dynamic ahead of the England team arriving in Miami for their game against New Zealand on the weekend 👀

BREAKING: 23-time Grand Slam winner Serena Williams is returning to tennis almost four years after her last competitive appearance to compete in the doubles event at the HSBC Championship at Queens Club in London 🚨

"Would you want to take it?" 

Craig Bellamy on what it means to be the Wales manager and his ambitions for the Euros in 2028 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿

"I'm not missing it!" 😅

Retired Italian jockey Frankie Dettori discusses his retirement 🏇

'More duels won and more possession won in final third of the pitch'

How Andoni Iraola's Liverpool could look in comparison to Arne Slot's ⤵️
BREAKING: Stockport part ways with head coach Dave Challinor after losing the League One play-off final to Bolton 🚨
The England squad are on their way to Miami for the World Cup 🏆

🚨 Liverpool are set to begin formal talks with Andoni Iraola over replacing Arne Slot as manager this week.

Sebastian Hoeneß and Pierre Sage are also in consideration.

🚨 Wrexham are interested in Sunderland goalkeeper Anthony Patterson, with Millwall and Wolves also keen. 

Sunderland are hoping for between £8-10m for the 26-year-old shot-stopper.
BREAKING: Lionesses captain Leah Williamson has been ruled out of England's next two World Cup qualifiers 🚨

"Amazing guy... Absolute role model to young people."

Harry Redknapp shares his thoughts on James Milner's career after he announced his retirement from football after 24 years in Premier League 💪

"Declan Rice was a lot of money at the time, but what a bargain he's turned out to be."

Harry Redknapp praised Arsenal's recruitment and says the Gunners will be "the team to beat again next year" 💪
Harry Redknapp says he would have liked to have seen Cole Palmer, Harry Maguire, Phil Foden and Jarrod Bowen called up to Thomas Tuchel's World Cup squad but believes England can "bring it home" 🏠
